# Matador

> Drop-in observability and operations control plane for BullMQ (the Redis-backed
> job queue for Node.js). One line of integration adds retry-aware latency and
> wait-time histograms, throughput/failure/retry counters, live queue-depth
> gauges, Prometheus and OTLP export, a dashboard, and safe fleet operations,
> with zero changes to job code and zero cost on the worker's processing path.

## What it is

- TypeScript monorepo, pnpm workspaces, published to npm under `@matador/*`.
- `@matador/core`: backend-neutral registry and one-line fail-open instrumentation.
- `@matador/prometheus`: Prometheus `MetricsSink` and a `/metrics` endpoint.
- Peer dependencies: `bullmq`, `ioredis`. License: AGPL-3.0-only.

## Design invariants

1. One-line integration; no job-code changes.
2. Neutral core; exporters implement a `MetricsSink` interface; core imports none.
3. Fail-open: instrumentation never throws into the worker or crashes the process.
4. Zero hot-path cost: durations from in-process worker events; gauges on a
   separate Redis connection.
5. Bounded cardinality: labels are `queue` and opt-in job `name` only.
6. Scrape-time gauges read getJobCounts behind a short-TTL single-flight cache.
7. One config object; nothing else reads the environment.

## Positioning

Matador does not recompete with BullMQ's built-in Prometheus export on basic
gauges; it interoperates with it. Its differentiators are correct retry-aware
wait-time and latency metrics, OTLP, and an open-source dashboard plus safe
operational actions.

## Key files

- Quick start and overview: README.md
- Security reporting: SECURITY.md
- Threat model: THREAT_MODEL.md
- Contributing and releasing: CONTRIBUTING.md, RELEASING.md
